title: WhatsApp Bots, Flows & Campaigns
lede: Go beyond the inbox: build native WhatsApp forms (Flows), deterministic chat bots, a shoppable product catalog, and bulk template campaigns with per-recipient personalization — all from one hub. This guide walks every power tab from A to Z.

steps.title: Open the WhatsApp hub and meet the power tabs
steps.content:
Everything WhatsApp lives on one page — and the tabs on the right side of its tab bar are where the automation magic happens.

  1. In the left sidebar, under Messages, click WhatsApp (or use the top navigation: Messages → WhatsApp).
  2. Look at the tab bar across the top of the page. This guide covers the five "power" tabs:
  • Flows — Meta-native, multi-screen forms that open inside WhatsApp (lead capture, appointment booking, sign-ups).
  • Catalog — your product catalog on WhatsApp: send product messages, receive orders, and track customer inquiries.
  • Bots — a visual builder for deterministic, scripted conversations that answer instantly without an LLM.
  • Lists — phone-number recipient lists that feed your campaigns.
  • Campaigns — bulk template sends with per-recipient personalization, pacing, scheduling, and a live report.
💡 The Messages, Numbers, and Templates tabs (the day-to-day inbox, connecting numbers, and creating message templates) are covered in the companion guide WhatsApp Business Messaging — see What's Next below.

images.alt: WhatsApp hub page with the Flows tab selected, showing the tab bar with Messages, Numbers, Templates, Flows, Catalog, Bots, Lists and Campaigns
images.caption: Messages → WhatsApp: the Flows, Catalog, Bots, Lists and Campaigns tabs are the power surface of the hub

images.alt: WhatsApp hub page with the Flows tab selected, showing the tab bar with Messages, Numbers, Templates, Flows, Catalog, Bots, Lists and Campaigns
images.caption: Messages → WhatsApp: the Flows, Catalog, Bots, Lists and Campaigns tabs are the power surface of the hub

steps.title: Prerequisite: connect a WhatsApp Business Account
steps.content:
Flows, Catalog, and Bots are scoped to a WhatsApp Business Account (WABA). Until one is connected, those three tabs show a friendly gate instead of their tools.

  1. If you see Connect a WhatsApp Business Account first, click Go to Numbers — or click the blue Connect WhatsApp button in the top-right corner (it is visible from every tab).
  2. Complete Meta's Embedded Signup in the popup: pick or create your WhatsApp Business Account and link a phone number.
  3. Once at least one number shows Ready to send on the Numbers tab, come back — the Flows, Catalog, and Bots tabs unlock automatically.
⚠️ If your business has multiple WABAs connected, each of these tabs shows its own Account picker at the top — flows, catalogs, and bots created under one Business Account do not appear under another, so always check which account is selected.

images.alt: Flows tab empty state saying Connect a WhatsApp Business Account first, with a Go to Numbers button
images.caption: Flows, Catalog and Bots wait behind this gate until a WhatsApp Business Account is connected

images.alt: Flows tab empty state saying Connect a WhatsApp Business Account first, with a Go to Numbers button
images.caption: Flows, Catalog and Bots wait behind this gate until a WhatsApp Business Account is connected

steps.title: Flows — build native in-chat forms
steps.content:
A WhatsApp Flow is a multi-screen form that opens inside the chat — no website, no friction. Customers book appointments, request quotes, or sign up without ever leaving WhatsApp.

  1. Open the Flows tab.
  2. Click New flow (green button, top-right of the toolbar). A draft is created instantly and the visual designer opens, where you add screens, fields, and buttons, rename the flow, and pick its categories.
  3. Already built flows in Meta's WhatsApp Manager? Click Sync to mirror them here.

Each row in the flows table shows:

  • Status — DRAFT (editable), PUBLISHED (sendable, locked), DEPRECATED (sunset), plus Meta-flagged BLOCKED / THROTTLED states. A red badge counts validation errors — open the designer to fix them.
  • Categories — e.g. Lead generation, Appointment booking, Sign up.
  • Responses — how many customers submitted the form; click the number to read every submission.

Per-row action icons (left to right): Preview in Meta (opens a live interactive preview), Analytics, Open visual designer, Quick edit (metadata + raw JSON), Publish (drafts only), Deprecate (published only), and Delete. Use the Status and Category filters or the search box to find a flow fast.

⚠️ Publishing is one-way: a PUBLISHED flow can be sent to customers but can never be edited again — you would clone it to make changes. Deleting a draft removes it at Meta too; deleting a published flow only removes your local copy.

steps.title: Bots — script instant, deterministic conversations
steps.content:
Bots answer in milliseconds and never improvise. They are perfect for menus, FAQs, opening hours, and structured intake — and they can run before or instead of your AI Smart Agent.

  1. Open the Bots tab and click New bot.
  2. Give it a name and description, then save — you land in the visual designer, where you chain message nodes, wait-for-reply nodes, branches, and variable captures into a conversation tree.
  3. Back in the list, use the per-row buttons:
    • Edit — reopen the visual designer.
    • Test (simulator) — chat with the bot right in a modal. It runs the exact program the live runner uses, but nothing is really sent — type a customer message, press Send, and inspect the reply plus a step-by-step trace.
    • Analytics — conversation stats for this bot.
    • Bind to phone numbers — attach the bot to one or more WhatsApp numbers (or SMS numbers, if the bot has the SMS channel enabled). A bound number runs the bot for every inbound message as the phone-level default.
    • Publish (lightning icon, drafts only) — make it live. Publishing is blocked while the bot has compile errors (a red badge shows the count).
    • Archive (published bots) — takes it offline and severs its phone bindings.
    • Delete — removes the bot and its bindings.
💡 Recommended rhythm: build → Test (simulator) until every branch behaves → Publish → Bind to phone numbers. A bot only answers real customers once it is both published and bound.

images.alt: Bots tab empty state explaining bots are scoped to a WhatsApp Business Account
images.caption: The Bots tab before a WABA is connected — once unlocked, New bot opens the visual conversation designer

images.alt: Bots tab empty state explaining bots are scoped to a WhatsApp Business Account
images.caption: The Bots tab before a WABA is connected — once unlocked, New bot opens the visual conversation designer

steps.title: Catalog — sell products directly in the chat
steps.content:
The Catalog tab turns WhatsApp into a storefront: customers browse your products, tap to inquire, and place orders that land straight in your CRM.

  1. Open the Catalog tab.
  2. Set up your first catalog with one of three buttons:
    • Create catalog — creates a product catalog under your Meta Business Manager, links it to this WABA, and sets it as the default, all in one click.
    • Link existing — attach a catalog you already have in your Business Manager.
    • Sync from Meta — mirror catalogs (and their products) you manage in Meta's Commerce Manager.
  3. Once a catalog is active, a stats banner tracks Catalogs, Synced products, Orders (30d), Inquiries (30d), and Revenue (30d), and four sub-tabs appear:
    • Products — browse, add, and edit the items in the selected catalog.
    • Orders — inbound WhatsApp orders, with open-order count badges; each can flow into your CRM pipeline.
    • Inquiries — a signal log of every product a customer tapped or asked about — warm leads on a silver platter.
    • Catalogs — manage all catalogs linked to this WABA and choose the default.
💡 The Commerce Manager button in the toolbar opens Meta's canonical catalog UI in a new tab — handy for bulk product uploads or anything the in-app editor doesn't cover. If you have more than one catalog, the Catalog: picker in the toolbar switches between them.

images.alt: Catalog tab empty state explaining catalog inventory and commerce signals are scoped to a WhatsApp Business Account
images.caption: The Catalog tab gate — after connecting, Create catalog wires a Meta catalog to your WABA in one click

steps.title: Lists — create a recipient list
steps.content:
Campaigns don't send to ad-hoc numbers — they send to recipient lists. Build the list once, reuse it for every blast.

  1. Open the Lists tab.
  2. Click New list (top-right of the Recipient lists card).
  3. Enter a Name (required — e.g. "Spring promo — leads with phone") and an optional Description, then click Create.

The table shows each list's Name, Description, Members count, and Created date. Click a list's name (or its Manage button) to open the members drawer, or the red trash icon to delete it.

images.alt: Lists tab showing the Recipient lists table with a demo list and the New list button
images.caption: The Lists tab — every campaign starts with a recipient list

images.alt: Lists tab showing the Recipient lists table with a demo list and the New list button
images.caption: The Lists tab — every campaign starts with a recipient list

images.alt: New recipient list modal with Name and Description fields
images.caption: New list: a name is all it takes

images.alt: New recipient list modal with Name and Description fields
images.caption: New list: a name is all it takes

steps.title: Fill the list — pull from your CRM or paste a CSV
steps.content:
Open a list (click its name) to manage members. Two import paths live in the drawer header:

Option A — Add from CRM (the smart way):

  1. Click Add from CRM.
  2. Switch between Contacts and Leads with the toggle at the top.
  3. Narrow the audience with the filters: Name or phone search, Tags, Accounts, State, City — and for leads, Status and Source. Click Apply.
  4. Tick individual rows and click Add selected, or click Add all matching to snapshot every record that matches the current filters (one recipient per phone number).

Option B — Paste CSV: click Paste CSV and paste one recipient per line in the format phone, first name, last name (e.g. +15551234567, Jane, Doe), then click Import. A header row starting with "phone" is ignored automatically.

  • Only records that have a phone number are shown in the picker — recipients without a phone are skipped at import (the success message tells you how many).
  • Each member row shows its Source (lead / contact / csv), and an opted out tag if that number has unsubscribed.
  • Select members and click Remove to prune the list; use the Search phone box to find someone fast.
💡 Opted-out numbers stay visible in the list but are skipped automatically at send time for marketing templates — you never have to clean them out by hand, and you stay compliant by default.

steps.title: Campaigns — build a bulk template send
steps.content:
This is the payoff: one approved template, thousands of personalized messages, delivered at exactly the pace you choose.

  1. Open the Campaigns tab and click New campaign.
  2. Fill in the builder drawer:
    • Campaign name — internal label, e.g. "Spring promo blast".
    • Recipient list — pick one of your lists (the member count shows next to each name).
    • Send from — the connected, registered WhatsApp number that will send.
    • Template — choose an approved template from that number's Business Account (templates load after you pick a number).
    • Send speed — 🐢 Trickle (300/hr), 🚶 Steady (3k/hr), 🏃 Fast (15k/hr), 🚀 Rapid (60k/hr), or ⚡ Max.
    • Schedule (optional) — pick a date and time, or leave empty to send immediately.
  3. If the template contains variables, a Template variables card appears. WhatsApp templates use positional slots like {{1}} — map each one to a CRM token ({first_name}, {last_name}, {full_name}, {phone}, {city}, {state}, {account_name}, or any custom field key) or type literal text. Tokens are filled per recipient at send time.
  4. If the template has an image, video, or document header, supply a public URL for the media (same file for all recipients; documents can also take a filename).
  5. Click Build & send to launch (or schedule) — or Save draft to finish later.
⚠️ Start with a slower speed tier on a fresh number. Meta assigns each number a daily messaging limit tier that grows with good engagement — blasting at ⚡ Max from day one risks quality-rating drops. For marketing-category templates, opted-out recipients are skipped automatically.

steps.title: Track, pause, and read the live report
steps.content:
Once launched, the campaign row keeps you in control:

  • Status — draft, scheduled, sending, paused, sent, or cancelled, with a live Progress bar.
  • Send — launches a draft (builds the recipient queue and starts sending).
  • Pause / Resume — freeze and continue a sending or scheduled campaign at any time.
  • Report — opens the live report drawer.
  • The trash icon deletes a draft, sent, or cancelled campaign.

Inside the Report drawer you get:

  1. A progress bar plus counters for Sent, Delivered, Read, Replied, Failed, Opted out, and Skipped (cooldown) — and once sending starts, live Delivery rate, Read rate, and Reply rate percentages.
  2. A per-recipient table with a status filter (queued, sending, sent, delivered, read, failed, opted_out, skipped) and a detail column showing timestamps, error messages, or "Must wait Xh before sending again" for frequency-capped numbers.
  3. A Retry failed button that re-queues every failed recipient in one click.
💡 "Skipped (cooldown)" is a built-in frequency cap protecting your number: a recipient who was messaged very recently is held back instead of spammed. Replies to your campaign land in the Messages tab like any other conversation — have your bot or Smart Agent ready to catch them, and the reply rate becomes revenue.

support.title: Need Help?
support.content: Questions about WhatsApp flows, bots, catalogs, or campaign sends? Contact our support team at support@imash.io and we'll help you get your first campaign out the door.
